## Target's compacted history
The target's earlier work has been folded into the `<summary>` block(s) below — this is what the target itself sees at the head of its context, NOT verbatim transcript. Use it to understand the arc: what was decided, what constraints were set, what was already done. If the window below shows the target acting on an assumption that CONTRADICTS a summary, re-doing work a summary says was completed, or ignoring a constraint a summary records — that is context drift; flag it. Do NOT trace values inside the summaries (they're compacted, not tool-verifiable). Do NOT emit `msg_idx` pointing into this section — `msg_idx` is window-relative. If a summary's paraphrase is ambiguous on the point you're checking, `query_target_history(summary=<id>, question=…)` fetches the original behind it — use sparingly.

<summary id=be3zj3fu>
DM verification, diagnostics, and Figure-1 prep — Faber2026 co-detection manuscript

## Narrative record

**Opening request (e3).** USER: "Confirm values and storage location for the most up-to-date DM fits / measurements. Point me to one of the diagnostic figures so I know you have them right."

**Locating the DM catalog (e5–e17).** Memory search confirmed the DM adoption policy context. A grep of the Faber2026 repo (`/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026`) found `dm_measurements_table.tex` at repo root, generated from `analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/manuscript_dm_catalog.csv`. The `.tex` and CSV match to the last digit; working tree clean; **v2 is the only version** (no v3), CSV last committed 2026-07-13 in commit `d555a6e` "manuscript: adopt verified phase-coherence DMs". Decision record: `docs/rse/specs/verified-dm-adoption-2026-07-13.md`. Upstream source of record: `dsa110-FLITS` branch `agent/dm-phase-v2` commit `c07f1f1660ee0736459eead04ebf69eaa82aebc4`, snapshot vendored under `analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/code/`. Adoption policy: CHIME/FRB primary for every burst (~34× cold-plasma DM leverage of 400–800 MHz over 1311–1499 MHz DSA band; narrower/more stable coherence curves); DSA retained as independent cross-check; adopted σ = CHIME band σ (folds channel-block jackknife, resolution, fluctuation-cutoff, 0.005 pc cm⁻³ numerical floor).

The full adopted-DM table (CHIME-primary, pc cm⁻³, with ΔDM = CHIME−DSA) is preserved verbatim in <assistant>I have them right. Here is the confirmation.

## Most up-to-date DM measurements — confirmed

The current, manuscript-adopted DMs are the **v2 phase-coherence** fits. They live here:

- **Authoritative catalog:** `analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/manuscript_dm_catalog.csv`
- **Manuscript table (generated from it):** `dm_measurements_table.tex` (repo root)
- **Decision record:** `docs/rse/specs/verified-dm-adoption-2026-07-13.md`
- **Upstream source of record:** `dsa110-FLITS`, branch `agent/dm-phase-v2`, commit `c07f1f166` (snapshot vendored under `analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/code/`)

I verified the CSV and the `.tex` table agree to the last digit, the working tree is clean, and **v2 is the only version present** — no v3, and the CSV was last committed 2026-07-13 (`d555a6e "manuscript: adopt verified phase-coherence DMs"`). The earlier adaptive-arrival / DM-phase-v1 `UNCONSTRAINED` results are explicitly retired by the decision record and their producer script was removed.

**Adoption policy:** CHIME/FRB is primary for every burst (its 400–800 MHz band carries ≈34× the cold-plasma DM leverage of the 1311–1499 MHz DSA band and its coherence curves are narrower/more stable); DSA-110 is retained as an independent cross-check; uncertainties fold in channel-block jackknife, resolution, fluctuation-cutoff, and a 0.005 pc cm⁻³ numerical floor.

**Adopted DMs (CHIME-primary, pc cm⁻³):**

| Burst | DM_adopted | ΔDM (CHIME−DSA) |
|---|---|---|
| FRB 20220207C (zach) | 262.3617 ± 0.0104 | +0.0453 |
| FRB 20220310F (whitney) | 462.1888 ± 0.0050 | −0.0040 |
| FRB 20220506D (oran) | 397.0155 ± 0.0050 | +0.0745 |
| FRB 20221113A (isha) | 411.4357 ± 0.0050 | −0.1170 |
| FRB 20221203A (wilhelm) | 602.3778 ± 0.0060 | −0.0014 |
| FRB 20230307A (phineas) | 610.2891 ± 0.0050 | +0.0565 |
| FRB 20230325A (freya) | 912.4076 ± 0.0157 | −0.0835 |
| FRB 20230814B (johndoeII) | 696.5180 ± 0.0050 | +0.0525 |
| FRB 20230913A (hamilton) | 518.7970 ± 0.0050 | −0.0007 |
| FRB 20240122A (mahi) | 960.1316 ± 0.0050 | +0.0458 |
| FRB 20240203A (chromatica) | 272.6387 ± 0.0119 | +0.0773 |
| FRB 20240229A (casey) | 491.2078 ± 0.0052 | +0.0103 |

## Diagnostic figure — pointer

Per-event diagnostics: `analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/results/diagnostics/<nick>_joint_dm_diagnostic.png`; all-event audit in `all_events_contact_sheet.jpg`; held-out recovery in `results/validation/injection_recovery.png`.

Here is the **phineas (FRB 20230307A)** diagnostic so you can confirm I'm reading the right products:

![phineas joint DM-phase diagnostic — CHIME (400–800 MHz) and DSA (1311–1499 MHz) dedispersed waterfalls, phase-coherence curves, cutoff/resolution stability, and leave-channel-block-out jackknife](phineas_joint_dm_diagnostic.png)

What this panel shows, and why it corroborates the table:
- **Top-left waterfalls** — CHIME product vs. best-DM (610.2891) and DSA product vs. best-DM (610.2325); the burst is vertical at the fitted DM in both bands.
- **Phase-coherence curves (top-right)** — the CHIME peak (blue) is markedly narrower than DSA (orange), which is exactly the reason CHIME is adopted as primary; the black "joint" line and shaded bands sit between them.
- **Bottom row** — cutoff sensitivity, native-resolution stability, and the leave-channel-block-out peak histograms feeding the jackknife term.
- **Footer** — `QUALITY PASS_SYSTEMATIC`, CHIME−DSA = +0.05653 ± 0.00868 (6.51σ), joint σ widened by a between-band systematic τ = 0.0395. This is why phineas carries the `PASS_SYSTEMATIC` flag in `summary.md`: both bands passed the fit-quality gates, but the shared uncertainty was inflated by the measured inter-band offset — it is **not** a failed fit. Note the adopted value still uses the CHIME σ (0.0050), not the widened joint σ.

One thing worth flagging for the manuscript: **phineas has a real 6.5σ CHIME−DSA DM discrepancy** (+0.057 pc cm⁻³). It's small in absolute terms and the CHIME-primary policy handles it, but if a referee scrutinizes ΔDM, phineas (and isha at −0.117) are the two sightlines where the between-band offset exceeds the quoted band errors. Want me to open the isha diagnostic too, or pull the full ΔDM-vs-significance breakdown across all twelve?</assistant>. Nicks↔TNS: zach=20220207C, whitney=20220310F, oran=20220506D, isha=20221113A, wilhelm=20221203A, phineas=20230307A, freya=20230325A, johndoeII=20230814B, hamilton=20230913A, mahi=20240122A, chromatica=20240203A, casey=20240229A. Two `PASS_SYSTEMATIC` sightlines have real between-band offsets: **phineas +0.0565 (6.5σ), isha −0.1170**. summary.md `PASS_SYSTEMATIC` flag (freya, isha, phineas, zach) = both bands passed fit-quality gates but joint σ widened by between-band scatter; NOT a failed fit. Diagnostic figure shown: phineas per-event, read_file version_id `025b98f9-09f8-4fec-8b2f-081f7d656d6e`.

**Diagnostics into canvas (e20–e34).** USER (e20): "let me see the diagnostics". USER (e25): "open the images in canvas to the right here". Copied three figures to workspace and saved as artifacts:
- `all_events_contact_sheet.jpg` → artifact_id `abdd8850-3584-4913-b903-20b626e17de6`, version_id `6221b5f3-0ee0-4582-a101-528467829858`
- `phineas_joint_dm_diagnostic.png` → artifact_id `acf484e8-9cb9-4c34-abda-eee76644c5e1`, version_id `025b98f9-09f8-4fec-8b2f-081f7d656d6e`
- `injection_recovery.png` → artifact_id `e68b7fc5-51c5-4903-860e-ef3263eaeaa6`, version_id `8a074177-8d20-47ab-b3a1-df904e71daf2`

Injection-recovery: CHIME bias +1×10⁻⁴, RMSE 3×10⁻⁴; DSA bias +5.2×10⁻³, RMSE 8.3×10⁻³; JOINT bias +1.9×10⁻³, RMSE 2.8×10⁻³ (~25× tighter CHIME).

**Coherence-curve comparison figure (e37–e57).** USER (e37): "I can't make out the necessary detail in the all-events figure. The phineas plot doesn't show clear differences either, but I'm partial to the CHIME measurement I think". Built a purpose-made 12-panel figure from `analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/results/fits.json` (a list of 12 dicts, keys `burst/chime/dsa/joint`; each band has `selected_curve` {dm_grid, score, cutoff_peaks_absolute, cutoff_contrast, jackknife_peaks_absolute}, `sigma_components` {jackknife, resolution, cutoff, floor}, `dm`, `sigma`, `product_dm`, `input_path`, `frequency_mhz`). Loaded `figure-style` skill; called `apply_figure_style(sizes=(8,7,6))`. Colors: CHIME `#1f5fbf` (adopted), DSA `#e08214` (cross-check). Computed per-band σ ratios and coherence FWHM. Fixed a self-consistency bug: freya/phineas showed "1× tighter" (misleading) → corrected format to show 1.4× for ratios <3. Saved `dm_coherence_chime_vs_dsa.png` → artifact_id `7d673c1a-0773-4e77-bf1a-6f9fa98cdfbc`, version_id `7c41a07e-51a9-4f64-aae5-24034f08ecbd`. Key result: CHIME peak narrower in all 12; σ_DSA/σ_CHIME 1.4×–17×, median 7.4×.

**Submodule placement request (e60).** USER: "Make sure this plot ends up in the Faber2026/pipeline/ submodule, not just as an artifact in this session." Explored `pipeline/` (the dsa110-FLITS submodule, url `https://github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS.git`, pinned at `b3f02a9950da0c286f83b3b7e29b7f38b06e95ee`). Chose `dispersion/dm_campaign/` as home. Found the v2 renderer `render_joint_dm_diagnostics.py` exists only in the snapshot (`analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/code/scripts/`), NOT in the pinned submodule; `dm_joint_phase.py` also absent from pin (only in snapshot); `dm_power_analysis.py` IS present in pin. Submodule `.gitignore` has `*.png` (line 139) — pipeline repo does not track PNGs; scripts are the tracked deliverable. Also confirmed the separately-granted FLITS repo checkout at `/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS` is on branch `main` HEAD `fed4a02c1b022b22a3853dbcca81e4e53c4910b4` (different from the submodule's HEAD).

**Dynamic-spectrum zoom pivot (e72).** USER: "I can only really assess which DM is correct based on a zoom-on of the burst in the dynamic spectrum." Verified all 24 raw Stokes-I waterfall products exist locally at `/Users/jakobfaber/Data/Faber2026/dsa110/DSA_bursts/<nick>_<band>_I_..._cntr_bpc.npy` (paths + product_dm in fits.json). Wrote a self-contained figure script `pipeline/dispersion/dm_campaign/render_dm_zoom_comparison.py` importing pipeline helpers (`CHIME_DT_S=2.56e-6`, `DSA_DT_S=3.2768e-5`, `_freq_grid_mhz`, `_orient_waterfall_to_ascending_frequency`, `shift_waterfall_residual_dm` from `dispersion.dm_power_analysis`) and **vendored verbatim** `normalise_channels` + `crop_on_pulse` from the v2 snapshot's `dm_joint_phase.py` (because that module is not in the pin) with a provenance comment citing FLITS branch agent/dm-phase-v2 commit c07f1f166.

**Import fix:** `dispersion` is a namespace package (no `__init__.py`); `-m` run needed `PYTHONPATH=.` from the pipeline root. py312 interpreter invoked directly as `/Users/jakobfaber/.conda/envs/py312/bin/python` (conda run panics). First probe (adopted mode, 2-col: each band at its own DM) rendered 4 bursts phineas/isha/hamilton/casey — revealed CHIME bursts look diffuse (scattering, τ∝ν⁻⁴, ~25–40× broadening at 0.6 GHz), DSA crisp; so "most vertical" naively favors DSA but that's scattering not DM error.

**Discriminate mode (e95–e104).** Added a 4-column discriminate render (`render_discriminate`): each band dedispersed at BOTH candidate DMs = CHIME@CHIME-DM, CHIME@DSA-DM, DSA@CHIME-DM, DSA@DSA-DM, with expected tilt annotated. Added helpers `K_DM_MS=4.148808` and `_sweep_ms(delta_dm, f_lo, f_hi)`. Wired `--mode {adopted,discriminate}` CLI. Computed the discriminating physics: a ΔDM tilts CHIME by hundreds of samples, DSA by 1–2 samples — the 429-vs-1-sample asymmetry IS the leverage argument. e104 (last event in span) is the bash run rendering the discriminate probe for phineas+isha at window-ms 10 (output filed).

Decisions:
- dm_adoption_policy = "I'm partial to the CHIME measurement" [project, ruling, by:user] (src: <f378b995>)
- figure_principle = "show the one quantity a claim rests on rather than dense multi-panel audits" [project, constraint, by:user] (src: <f378b995>)
- dm_which_correct_test = "I can only really assess which DM is correct based on a zoom-on of the burst in the dynamic spectrum" [project, ruling, by:user] (src: <83e54d84>)
- zoom_script_location = "Make sure this plot ends up in the Faber2026/pipeline/ submodule, not just as an artifact in this session" [project, process, by:user] (src: <b4e96ed3>)

Open threads:
- Figure-1 generation is the NEXT request (arriving after e104): regenerate Figure 1 with correct DMs and remove the manuscript placeholder. Figure 1 = `fig:codetection-data-grid` (twelve-burst CHIME/DSA gallery) at `sections/observations.tex:37`, currently an `[AUTHOR REVIEW PENDING --- FIGURE 1]` fbox placeholder gated by SHA-256 figure-review packet (`figure_review/slots.json` + README, Section sec:multicomp). Existing machinery: `scripts/plot_codetection_data_grid.py`, `tests/test_codetection_data_grid.py`, `figures/codetection_data_grid.{pdf,png,svg}`, branch `ms/figure1-data-grid`.
- Tilt (3rd) mode, PR creation (FLITS #185, Faber2026 #83), and promotion of figures into tracked `figures/` all occur AFTER this span.
- Discriminate/adopted probe figures rendered to `/tmp/dm_zoom_probe/`; not yet promoted to tracked locations at span end.

**3.7 Physical width.** A single-row figure at 300 dpi fits the venue's
This is the DM-leverage argument made visible — the 400–800 MHz band has ~34× the cold-plasma dispersive delay of the 1.3–1.5 GHz DSA band.
The key physics: a ΔDM of 0.057 pc cm⁻³ produces a ~1.1 ms sweep across CHIME's 400–800 MHz band (very visible) but only ~30 µs across DSA's 1.3–1.5 GHz band (≈1 sample, invisible).
